questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

Financial inclusion get a leg up(A)/ with 155,000 post offices potentially becoming(B)/ bank branches empowering the poor to access(C)/ financial services in the hinterland that has been untouched by banks.(D)
opts: Financial inclusion get a leg up
bank branches empowering the poor to access
Financial services in the hinterland that has been untouched by banks.
questions.sol:
Answer: A
Singular noun 'Financial inclusion' needs singular verb 'gets' not 'get'.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

The existing SEZ Act was(A)/ enacted in 2006 with an aim to(B)/ creating export hubs and boost(C)/ manufacturing in the country(D).
opts: enacted in 2006 with an aim to
questions.sol:
Answer: C
Infinitive 'to' takes base form of verb. 'creating' must be replaced by 'create'.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

While it acknowledged that(A)/ learning among children has been(B)/affected because of prolong(C)/ periods of school closures(D).
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'prolong' must be replaced with adjective 'prolonged' as it modifies the noun 'periods'.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

The National Security Council Secretariat (NSCS)(A)/, which is headed by National Security Advisor (NSA)(B)/ Ajit Doval has been allocated rs. 232.71 crore(C)/, against rs.228.72 crore allocate last year(D).
opts: which is headed by National Security Advisor (NSA)
Ajit Doval has been allocated rs. 232.71 crore
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'allocate' must be replaced with past form 'allocated' to match the tense.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

The resource crunch manifesto in(A)/ the proposed higher debt issuance is ultimate(B)/ bound to get more acute in the days ahead(C)/, given the Budget's lack of growth invigorating proposals (D).
opts: the proposed higher debt issuance is ultimate
bound to get more acute in the days ahead
given the Budget's lack of growth invigorating proposals
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'Ultimate' must be replaced with adverb 'ultimately' as it modifies the verb 'Bound'.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

The move comes in the(A)/ backdrop of China setting off(B)/ "model villages" close to(C)/ the Line of Actual Control (LAC) (D).
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'set off' (to leave on a journey) must be replaced by 'set up' (to establish).

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

The second set of appeals had been(A)/ filed against the High Court order(B)/ declining to stay an arbitration tribunal(C)/ decision refusing to interfere with the EA award(D).
opts: The second set of appeals had been
filed against the High Court order
declining to stay an arbitration tribunal
decision refusing to interfere with the EA award
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error.
The sentence is free from error.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

Last year, the share of(A)/ the health sector was 2.14%(B)/ and this year it is 2.19%(C)/. Its almost the same,” he said(D).
opts: Its almost the same,” he said.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'Its' (possessive) must be replaced by 'It’s' (contraction of 'it is').

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

Tokyo has sought to walk a(A)/ careful line in its approach to(B)/ China, balance the pressure put on(C)/ Beijing by close ally Washington(D).
opts: Tokyo has sought to walk a
careful line in its approach to
China, balance the pressure put on
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'balance' must be replaced by 'balancing' (gerund) after a comma continuing the clause.

questions.q:
In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.

In October 2018, India agreed to(A)/ purchase five Russian S-400 self-propelled(B)/ surface-to-air system for approximately $5.4 billion(C)/ during a state visit by president Vladimir Putin(D).
opts: during a state visit by president Vladimir Putin.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'system' must be replaced by plural 'systems' (five systems).

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: The applications will be until February 28, 2022.
Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman, presenting her fourth Budget and the second one in an pandemic hit economy on Tuesday.
Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman on Tuesday proposed a 30% tax on income from virtual digital assets, such as cryptocurrencies and nonfungible tokens (NFTs).
For businesses, the tax proposals are something of a mixed bag.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'an pandemic' is incorrect. 'Pandemic' starts with a consonant sound, so 'a pandemic' is correct.

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: The private industry is to be encouraged to take a part in R&D work.
68% of the defence budget is embarked for domestic procurement
The government has recognized crypto currencies and the RBI will introduce introduce a Digital Rupee.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — All are correct.
All sentences are free from error.

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: If I were you I would accept their offer.
As a result, 'we are people' would like to call it a landmark decision.
They have negligible expert in international law.
Certain important changes relating to deductibility of expenses are also proposed.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'Expert' must be replaced by 'Expertise' (high level of special knowledge or skill).

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: The high court, in appeal, while confirming the bail of Allen, chose to set aside the bail granted to Thwaha.
The former approach is reflected in Zahoor Ahmad Shah Watali and the later in Thwaha Fasal.
This provision is being made more rigorous by restricting the testing period to one year.
Such dividends will now be taxed at normal rates.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'Later' must be replaced by 'Latter' (referring to the second of two things already mentioned).

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: A healthy and productive workforce will be the life force of all our plans to build the India of the future.
We truly have a long way to go in build health resilience.
An outlay for health is an investment in our collective aspiration.
The future of health is exciting, with the seamless integration of physical resources with digital platforms.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
A preposition always takes gerund (verb+ing). 'Build' must be replaced by 'Building'.

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: The applications for digital tools across different independent functions of the 'smart city' have been catching up of late.
We also find adoption in China to be quite interesting, like the new Pudong area in Shanghai.
Faster mobile networks are at the heart of smart cities and are enabling people to do more on the move.
A small child is accidentally separated with his family and and ends up thousands of miles away from home.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'Separated with' must be replaced by 'Separated from' (correct preposition usage).

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: The first collection for Fall Winter is heavy and has a lot of trenches, jackets, trousers, skirts, and dresses in olives, browns, blues, and pinks.
The tax revenues for FY23 have been estimated in a very conservative manner.
The 30% tax on the sale of digital assets is very good.
The increase in defense purchases domestically from 58% by 68% indicates that the government means business here.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'from 58% by 68%' is incorrect. Correct phrase is 'from 58% to 68%'.

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: The budget for FY 2022-23 has been presented in unique circumstances.
The budget's main focus is on increasing capital expenditures for expanding the economic infrastructure under the PM Gati Shakti scheme.

Covid resulted in an over 30 percent shortfall of coverage under all these programmes giving rise to fears of drug-resistant HIV and tuberculosis and left lakhs of children unprotected from vaccine-preventable diseases.
An important takeaway ought to have been greater investment in health research.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
Parallelism error: 'left' must be replaced by 'leaving' to match 'giving'.

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: When he considered experts, they told him to go to some fitness centers.
Role of heightened awareness regarding health and lifestyle, are also significant in the growth of the wellness industry.
The drone industry, already opened to private enterprise, has been given a huge captive market by integrating it with agriculture.
The pandemic created its own problems.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
Subject 'Role' is singular, so 'are' must be replaced by 'is'.

questions.q: Choose the incorrect sentence as the answer. If there is no error, the answer is 'option E'. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).
opts: India's growth is the highest among all major economies; we are now in a strong position to withstand challenges.
ECLGS cover expanded from Rs 50,000 to Rs 5 lakh crore.
Initiatives from last year's budget has been provided adequate allocations in this budget
Digital ecosystem for skilling and livelihood to be launched.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
Plural subject 'initiatives' needs plural verb 'have' not 'has'.

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts, from the given six parts two parts are error free and remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which do not contain any error.

Ritika Phogat hang herself (A)/from a ceiling fan at her (B)/ uncle's house in the (C)/ night of March as she were upset (D)/ over losing to her rival by one point from the (E)/ final bout of a championship, police said.(F)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — BF
A: 'hang'→'hanged' | C: 'in'→'on' | D: 'were'→'was' | E: 'from'→'in'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts, from the given six parts two parts are error free and remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which do not contain any error.

Even as most of last year saw (A)/Indian and Chinese soldiers engaging in an eyeball-eyeball (B)/ standoff near LAC in eastern Ladakh and violent clash (C)/ among troops in Galwan valley, China continued to (D)/ remained on top of the list of countries from where (E)/ India imported goods during January-December, 2020. (F)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — AF
B: 'engaging'→'engaged' | C: 'clash'→'clashes' | D: 'among'→'between' | E: 'remained'→'remain'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts, from the given six parts two parts are error free and remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which do not contain any error.

Gadkari unveiled the much-awaited (A)/ Voluntary Vehicle-Fleet Modernization Program (B) /or "Vehicle Scrapping Policy" which are (C)/ aimed on creating an ecosystem (D)/ for phasing out of unfitted and (E) /polluted vehicles, in the Lok Sabha. (F)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — AB
C: 'are'→'is' | D: 'on'→'at' | E: 'unfitted'→'unfit' | F: 'polluted'→'polluting'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts, from the given six parts two parts are error free and remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which do not contain any error.

The criteria of a vehicle to (A)/ be scrapped is primarily based on (B)/ the fitness of vehicles through (C)/Automatic Fitness Centers in case of (D)/commercial vehicles and Non-Renewal of Registration (E)/ in case of private vehicles, Gadkari say. (F)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — CE
A: 'of'→'for' | B: 'is'→'are' | D: 'Automatic'→'Automated' | F: 'say'→'said'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts, from the given six parts two parts are error free and remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which do not contain any error.

India Inc wants to went (A)/ back to office as much as 70% (B)/ of employers will not continue (C)/ remote working post-pandemic, (D)/with 59% employer not favoring (E) remote working in the new normalcy. (F)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — CD
A: 'went'→'go' | B: 'much'→'many' | E: 'employer'→'employers' | F: 'normalcy'→'normal'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts, from the given six parts two parts are error free and remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which do not contain any error.

The PM said 70 districts has (A)/ logged a over 150% increase (B)/ in Covid-19 cases recently, (C)/ and that "quick and decision steps" were (D)/ needed to revamp safety protocols, (E)/ scale up RT-PCR testing, and add more vaccinating sites. (F)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — CE
A: 'has'→'have' | B: 'a'→'an' | D: 'decision'→'decisive' | F: 'vaccinating'→'vaccination'

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into five parts out of which part A has been given without any error. Remaining some parts may or may not contain error. You have to identify the parts which do not have an error.

Most animals which live (A) / in crowding conditions have (B) / particular robust immune systems, (C) /so it long puzzled entomologists (D)/ that honeybees do not. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — DE
B: 'crowding'→'crowded' | C: 'particular'→'particularly'

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into five parts out of which part A has been given without any error. Remaining some parts may or may not contain error. You have to identify the parts which do not have an error.

Airbus expects its new plane (A)/ to be powered by hydrogen directly (B)/ as a fuel in new turbofans (C)/ of the sort now founded (D)/ in largest passenger jets. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — BC
D: 'founded'→'found' | E: 'largest'→'large'

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into five parts out of which part A has been given without any error. Remaining some parts may or may not contain error. You have to identify the parts which do not have an error.

In November the European Commission accused (A)/ Amazon of violating competition laws (B)/ for using non-public data from (C)/ third-party sellers to benefit (D)/ itself own retail business. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — BD
C: 'for'→'by' | E: 'itself'→'its'

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into five parts out of which part A has been given without any error. Remaining some parts may or may not contain error. You have to identify the parts which do not have an error.

The growing ease and falling cost of travel, (A)/ plus a lack of any regulations (B)/ to curb emissions, means that unchecked a rose (C)/ to as much as 9% of total carbon (D)/ emissions by 2050 had looked plausible. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — BD
C: 'rose'→'rise' (noun needed after article) | E: 'had'→'has'

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into five parts out of which part A has been given without any error. Remaining some parts may or may not contain error. You have to identify the parts which do not have an error.

Globally the supermarket and superstore sector has (A)/ had a profitable pandemic, benefiting both from the fact (B)/ that grocery stores remained open during lockdowns, (C)/ and from the biggest surge in online activity of (D)/ any retail category-rising by almost 50% in America in 2020 (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The given sentence is entirely error free.

questions.q:
In each of the following questions a sentence is given which is divided into four parts. Some parts have an error in it. Identify the parts without any errors. If the whole sentence is error free, choose E as the answer.

Even though more than a quarter-century has elapsed since(A)/ the Constitution were amended to make urban and rural local bodies (B)/a self contained third tiers of governance, it is often (C)/agreed by experts that there is inadequate devolution of powers to them(D)/No Error(E).
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Only A&D
B: 'were'→'was' | C: 'tiers'→'tier'

questions.q:
In each of the following questions a sentence is given which is divided into four parts. Some parts have an error in it. Identify the parts without any errors. If the whole sentence is error free, choose E as the answer.

The mass exodus of migrant labourers in foot and the subsequent (A)/ running of Shramik Express trains by the Ministry of Railways in the middle of the COVID19 lockdown (B)/ and the logistical challenge in dealing with China figured as a recurring theme as the Lok Sabha (C)/took up the debate on demand of grants from the Railways (D)/No Error (E).
questions.sol:
Answer: B — Only B
A: 'in foot'→'on foot' | C: 'challenge'→'challenges' | D: 'from'→'for'

questions.q:
In each of the following questions a sentence is given which is divided into four parts. Some parts have an error in it. Identify the parts without any errors. If the whole sentence is error free, choose E as the answer.

The judgment echoes the arguments that child rights activists has been (A)/making for year by ignoring the natural sexual tendencies(B)/ of adolescents, POCSO can and does became tool for the persecution(C)/ of young people in consenting sexual relations(D)/No Error(E).
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Only C&D
A: 'has'→'have' | B: 'year'→'years'

questions.q:
In each of the following questions a sentence is given which is divided into four parts. Some parts have an error in it. Identify the parts without any errors. If the whole sentence is error free, choose E as the answer.

The Centre has informed a parliamentary panel (A)/ that a standalone project to build a (B)/critical road along the border with(C) China in Ladakh is going on (D)/No Error.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
All parts are error free.

questions.q:
In each of the following questions a sentence is given which is divided into four parts. Some parts have an error in it. Identify the parts without any errors. If the whole sentence is error free, choose E as the answer.

The government on Monday informed the Lok Sabha (A)/that 2,000 currency notes has not been printed in the past (B)/two years even as the quantum of the highest (C)/denomination currency note has came down(D)/No Error(E).
questions.sol:
Answer: A — A&C
B: 'has'→'have' | D: 'came'→'come'

questions.q: A sentence has been divided in five parts in each question. Select the grammatically correct sentence and mark the option accordingly.
opts: The portion of the interest earned from the state university's endowment
that is set aside to fund merit scholarships to outstanding
high school seniors is more numerous than that set aside to fund
questions.sol:
Answer: A
B: 'to outstanding'→'for outstanding' | C: 'more numerous'→remove 'more' (portion is uncountable) | D: 'high-power'→'high-powered'

questions.q: A sentence has been divided in five parts in each question. Select the grammatically correct sentence and mark the option accordingly.
opts: The public's expectation of what they hope
to learn from a visit to the museum is quite different
nowadays of the intentions with which
questions.sol:
Answer: D — museums in India were created
A: 'expectation'→'expectations' | B: 'the museum'→'a museum' | C: 'of'→'to' (different to)

questions.q: A sentence has been divided in five parts in each question. Select the grammatically correct sentence and mark the option accordingly.
opts: The fundamental point thus being established is
the veracity of consciousness when it does not go
beyond itself, or do not postulate
something which are external to itself
questions.sol:
Answer: B
A: 'being'→remove (simple present needed) | C: 'do'→'does' | D: 'are'→'is'

questions.q: A sentence has been divided in five parts in each question. Select the grammatically correct sentence and mark the option accordingly.
opts: In 1566 Victor was summoned after a newly erected tribunal
and condemned to death against gross neglect of duty,
though not one of the frivolous charges brought
questions.sol:
Answer: C
A: 'after'→'before' | B: 'against'→'for' | D: 'for'→'against'

questions.q: A sentence has been divided in five parts in each question. Select the grammatically correct sentence and mark the option accordingly.
opts: The present dearth of referral practices
North of the border makes this the
questions.sol: Answer: E — All are correct.

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into four parts out of which one part has an error in it. Find the part with error and mark that as your answer.

Three decades after it's economy (A) / was liberalized, India remains (B) / a complex and challenging (C) / place to do business. (D)/ No error.
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'it’s' (it is) must be replaced by 'its' (possessive).

questions.q:
In the question below, a sentence is divided into four parts out of which one part has an error in it. Find the part with error and mark that as your answer.

The much-awaited vehicle scrap page policy announced by the Transport Ministry, (A)/ coming after the move for a green tax (B)/ on ageing and polluting automobiles, (C)/ promising economic benefit. (D)/ No error.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'promising'→'promises'. Singular subject 'policy' needs singular verb 'promises'.

questions.q:
In the following questions a sentence is provided which is divided into different parts. One of these parts may or may not be grammatically or contextually correct. Choose the option corresponding to the error as part of your answer.

(A)The CBI registered a case against the Bofors president./(B) alleged middleman Win Chadha, Italian businessman/(C) Ottavio Quattrocchi and the London-based Hinduja /(D) brothers for crime conspiracy, cheating and forgery/(E)No errors
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'crime' (noun) must be replaced by adjective 'criminal' — criminal conspiracy.

questions.q: (A)Bihar is more than just a state./(B) It has been home for dynasties/(C)and kings and has emerged as/(D) a political hub over the years. /(E)No errors
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'home for' must be replaced by 'home to' (correct idiom — place where something belongs).

questions.q: (A)As on March 31, 2018, the Bank /(B)of Baroda assets from its international/(C) operations amounted to Rs. 1,667.13 billion,/(D) being 22.12% of Bank's global assets. /(E)No errors
questions.sol:
Answer: B
Two nouns cannot stand together without a possessive. 'Baroda assets' → 'Baroda's assets'.

questions.q: (A)Assam will showcase the state's bamboo /(B) and cane crafts in its tableau. Bamboo and cane are/(C) abundant and a integral part of the/(D) lifestyle and economy of Assam /(E)No errors
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'a integral' → 'an integral' (article 'an' before vowel sound).

questions.q: (A)With just a day left for the ceremony, /(B)participants are busy with last-minute preparations - from/(C) giving finishing touches to the colorful models, fixing the costumes/(D) and rehearsing the dance steps. /(E) No errors
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q:
In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct. If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E 'No Correction required' as the answer.

In a sale and leaseback model, an airline acquire the aircraft in an attractive price and sells it to a lessor and leases it back for its use.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Acquires the aircraft at
Subject 'airline' (singular) needs 'acquires'; correct preposition for price is 'at'.

questions.q: Excellent in education depend on the quality of mentorship and not on the size or location of campuses.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — Excellence in education depends
'Excellent' (adjective) → 'Excellence' (noun, subject); singular subject needs 'depends'.

questions.q: Some may looked upon his conduct as unpardonable indiscipline while others could endorse it on the ground that an "unethical" government deserved such treatment.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — Look upon his conduct
After modal 'may', base form 'look' is needed; 'conduct' is uncountable.

questions.q: There is a need for a deeper understanding on both sides of the sensitivities of the India-Bangladesh relationship, even as it's many successes are count and celebrated.
questions.sol:
Answer: A — Its many successes are counted
'it's' → 'its' (possessive); 'count' → 'counted' (passive past participle).

questions.q: A key problem in most engineering colleges is that the students should not only become skilled engineers but should also train to speak and write clearly.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No Correction required
The highlighted part is grammatically correct as written.

questions.q:
In the following questions, some parts of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence does not have an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'E'.

The Delhi High Court, which had resumed (A)/ full-fledged physical listening from March 15, decided to (B)/ again adapt the virtual mode of proceedings from April 9 to April 23 (C)/ in view of an alarming raise in COVID-19 cases in the national capital.(D)/ No error (E)
opts: The Delhi High Court, which had resumed
Full-fledged physical listening from March 15, decided to
Again adapt the virtual mode of proceedings from April 9 to April 23
In view of an alarming raise in COVID-19 cases in the national capital.
questions.sol:
Answer: A
B: 'listening'→'hearing' | C: 'adapt'→'adopt' | D: 'raise'→'rise'

questions.q: The three accused who were arrested (A)/ had an illegal stock of the injection. (B)/ They were selling it at Rs 10,500 which is much higher (C)/ than its original MRP. 11 injection bottles have been seized. (D)/ No error (E)
opts: The three accused who were arrested
Had an illegal stock of the injection.
They were selling it at Rs 10,500 which is much higher
Than its original MRP. 11 injection bottles have been seized.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is entirely error free.

questions.q: The step were taken as patients with (A)/ presumptive Covid-19 diagnosis was (B)/ neither getting treatment at Covid hospitals because of their negative (C)/ test report or at non-Covid centres for having Covid symptoms. (D)/ No error (E)
opts: The step were taken as patients with
Neither getting treatment at Covid hospitals because of their negative
Test report or at non-Covid centres for having Covid symptoms.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
A: 'were'→'was' | B: 'was'→'were' | D: 'or'→'nor' (neither...nor)

questions.q: Saudi Arabia's latest economic plan come (A)/ with a big risk as the plan might help to boost investment, (B)/ while on the other hand it could (C)/ also hitted the government's finances. (D)/ No error (E)
opts: Saudi Arabia's latest economic plan come
With a big risk as the plan might help to boost investment,
While on the other hand it could
questions.sol:
Answer: C
A: 'come'→'comes' | B: remove 'to' after help | D: 'hitted'→'hit'

questions.q: Doctors performed a 10-hours-long (A)/ domino liver transplant on the kids in which (B)/ one of the recipients become the donor (C)/ and the first recipient gets a health donor liver. (D)/ No error (E)
opts: Domino liver transplant on the kids in which
One of the recipients become the donor
And the first recipient gets a health donor liver.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
A: 'hours'→'hour' | C: 'become'→'becomes' | D: 'health'→'healthy'

questions.q:
In the questions below, a sentence is divided into four parts out of which two parts are error free. The remaining two parts have errors in it. Find out the error free parts.

The Kerala Police arrested Mr. Narayanan (a)/ based on suspicion that he was among those (b) /sharing official secrets relating by space (c) /technology and missions from foreign agents (d)/ No error(e).
questions.sol:
Answer: B — ab
c: 'relating by'→'relating to' | d: 'from'→'to'

questions.q: We calculate the rate of increase of new case (a)/ by looking at the daily new cases of the first (b)/ and second waves at time points (c)/ when the daily numbers was nearly equal (d)/No error(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — bc
a: 'case'→'cases' | d: 'was'→'were'

questions.q: The Centre had applied by the Supreme Court (a)/ urging it to accept on record the inquiry report submitted (b)/ by the Justice Jain Committee and had taken suitable actions on (c)/ the suggestions made within against the "erring officials" (d)/No error (e)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — bd
a: 'by'→'to' | c: 'had taken'→'take'

questions.q: China tried to change the status quo in Eastern Ladakh (a)/ by the use of disruptive technologies without using force(b)/ and thought that India as a nation will "succumb" (c)/ to the pressure due to their technological advantage (d)/ No error (e)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — ab
c: 'will'→'would' | d: 'their'→'its'

questions.q: The Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) will not be required (a)/ to register by a fresh OCI card every (b)/time a new passport is issued in (c) /there name, the Home Ministry said. (d)/ No error (e)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — ac
b: 'by'→'for' | d: 'there'→'their'

questions.q:
In the following questions, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence which has error/errors and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'E'.

The suspension of CAI comes at a time when the EU (A)/ has decided to reopen trade and investment negotiations (B)/ with India after a long hiatus of eight years, (C)/ primarily because a high ambition bar was maintained for New Delhi. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is entirely error free.

questions.q: India has been badly hitted by the second wave (A)/ of coronavirus and hospitals in several states (B)/ are reeling under shortage of health workers, (C)/ vaccines, oxygen, drugs and beds. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — Only A
'hitted'→'hit' (verb 'hit' is the same in all forms).

questions.q: The Spain has lifted a state of emergency in place (A)/ in October to fight the pandemic, (B)/ allowing Spaniards to travel between regions (C)/ for the first time in months. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — AB
A: remove 'the' before Spain | B: 'in'→'since'

questions.q: The opposition BJP in Chhattisgarh criticized (A)/ the ruling Congress on the decision, claiming that it seems (B)/ the state government priority was to provide liquor to people (C)/ instead of medical facilities during the COVID-19 pandemic. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — BC
B: 'on'→'over' | C: 'government'→'government's'

questions.q: Maruti Suzuki India on Sunday announced that (A)/ the company will boost manufacturing of Oxygen PSA generate plants. (B)/ Maruti has joined hands with two NCR-based companies (C)/ to boost its production of such plants. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — BD
B: 'generate'→'generator' | D: 'its'→'their'

questions.q:
Given below is a sentence divided into five parts. The bold part is without error. Determine the parts which are error-free and choose the option accordingly.

Quoting the latest soaring in profits(A)/ by huge computer companies, economists think that (B)/a cycle for computer users has begun in which they, (C)/ especially wall-street consumers, are (D)/ replacing their PC's with more powerful models. (E)/
questions.sol:
Answer: C — BDE
C: 'they' has no reference; should be 'a cycle has begun in which personal computer users'.

questions.q: A statistical model for studying (A)/ the inconsistencies of time (B)/ must be evaluated (C) / at a reasonable (D) / period in time. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — BC
D: 'at'→'over' (idiomatic: 'over a reasonable') | E: 'in'→'of' ('period of time').

questions.q: The army used the submarine(A)/ accident of the coast (B)/ of kozikhode to show (C)/ that everyone must be (D)/ held responsible for their actions. (E)/
questions.sol:
Answer: B — CD
B: 'of'→'off' (off the coast) | E: 'their'→'his/her' (everyone is singular).

questions.q: In a surprising study, BBC-TV emphasizes(A)/ on these people without optimism, (B)/ whose bodies are cared so by welfare aid, (C) / but whose spirit is often (D)/ ignored by a disinterested society. (E)/
questions.sol:
Answer: C — BDE
C: 'cared so by'→'cared for by' (verb 'care' always followed by 'for').

questions.q: If you are in a four-week(A)/ software making internship (B)/and, in two weeks, you put (C)/ together a program that solved (D)/ part of the problem, show it to your boss without delay. (E)/
questions.sol:
Answer: D — BE
C: 'put'→'have put' (present perfect needed) | D: 'solved'→'solves' (present, not past).

questions.q:
In each of the following questions, a sentence is divided into four parts; (A), (B), (C) and (D). There may be an error in one part, which makes the sentence grammatically or contextually incorrect. Choose the option with the part containing the error.

This is the first time that the Centre (A)/ has scrapped the notified interest rates over (B)/ small savings schemes after switching to a (C)/ quarterly interest rate setting system in April 2016.(D)/ No error (E).
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'over'→'on' (interest rates on small savings schemes).

questions.q: Vistara is planning to extend its gate-to-gate luggage (A)/ delivery service, under which check-in luggage is taken from passenger's home (B)/ before the journey and delivered to its final residing place, (C)/ to specific flights connecting Hyderabad as well as Bengaluru.(D)/ No error (E).
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'its'→'their' (referring to plural passengers).

questions.q: BhartiAirtel on March 31, said it has been empanelling (A)/ by Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-IN), which will allow (B)/ the company to offer its cyber security solutions to (C)/ Union and State governments, along with public sector entities. (D)/ No error (E).
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'empanelling'→'empanelled' (present perfect passive requires past participle).

questions.q: The two pilot unions of national carrier Air India (A)/ have urged the Minister of Civil Aviation and the airline's chairman and managing director (B)/ to roll back the reductions in their salary and reinstate (C)/ their monthly flying allowances in line with industry standards. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q:
In each question below, a sentence is divided into five parts out of which, part A is in bold and is error free. Among the remaining four parts two parts have an error in it and two parts are error free. Find out the error free parts.

While brands face issues such as (A) / the cost and availability of sustainable materials, (B) / more than half of the industry players want (C) / at least half of its products to (D)/ be made with sustainable materials in 2025. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — BC
D: 'its'→'their' (plural 'players') | E: 'in'→'by' (by 2025).

questions.q: Rather than introducing (A)/ any new rules on content, the new policy (B)/ essentially codifies and regulates (C)/ the companies existing practices (D)/ and introduces more transparent. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — BC
D: 'companies'→'company’s' (possessive) | E: 'transparent'→'transparency'.

questions.q: Around 18 percent of stores were closed on average during the year, (A)/ resulting in an annual net loss of €54 million, (B)/ but the global retail landscape recovered to pre-pandemic levels (C)/ in the second half of the year, (D)/ thanks to its digital push and store re-openings. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is entirely error free.

questions.q: Since the advent of the Covid-19 pandemic, (A)/ there have been numerous report (B)/ of fake sanitizers, handrubs (C)/ and N95 masks been confiscated (D)/ from different parts of the country. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — CE
B: 'report'→'reports' | D: 'been'→'being' (being confiscated).

questions.q: Formulation of a (A)/ national e-commerce policies is a move as it will (C)/ serves as a reference point (D)/ for the online retail industry. (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — CE
B: 'policies'→'policy' (article 'a' needs singular noun) | D: 'serves'→'serve' (modal + base verb).

questions.q:
In the following questions, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence does not have an error / errors and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'E'.

The Delhi government has previously written to the Centre to open (A)/ vaccination for all adults irrespective of (B)/ co-morbid conditions. It also said that it has the capacity to vaccinate (C)/ the national capital population within three months. (D) No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — BC
A: 'has'→'had' | D: 'capital'→'capital’s'.

questions.q: Tehran's announcement of stepped-up enrichment (A)/ has casted a shadow over talks in Vienna aimed at salvaging the 2015 (B)/ nuclear deal between Iran and world powers that than US president Donald Trump (C)/ abandoned almost three years ago. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — AD
B: 'casted'→'cast' | C: 'than'→'then'.

questions.q: Given that Beijing's energy and trade comes (A)/ from the west in the waterways (B)/ of the Indian Ocean, it would not be "surprising" to see (C)/ the Chinese come deeper into the Indian Ocean region.(D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — CD
A: 'comes'→'come' (plural subject) | B: 'in'→'through'.

questions.q: A small and growing number of colleges (A)/ will require students to receive a Covid-19 vaccine, (B)/ saying it is the most assured away of returning to (C)/ some semblance of pre-pandemic campus life.(D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — BD
A: 'and'→'but' | C: 'away'→'way'.

questions.q: On April 1, the DTP placed more than 600 cones (A)/ at the Delhi Gate intersection to improve road safety for pedestrians and commuters. (B)/ The cones were tightened with ropes and placed near zebra-crossings (C)/ to reduce accidents and ensure smooth movement of traffic. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is entirely error free.

questions.q:
Read each sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical or idiomatic error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. The number of that part will be the answer. If there is no error the answer is (E).

Five boys has been arrested by (A)/ the police in connection with the murder (B)/ of a male (C)/ foreign company earlier this month (D)/ No Error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A
Plural subject 'five boys' needs 'have' not 'has'.

questions.q: The second coronavirus wave in India has resulted in some additional health (A)/ complications for Covid patients. Experts are warning (B)/ about a possible link between Covid-19 or (C)/ gangrene as India deals with infections such as black fungus. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'between...or'→'between...and' (between always pairs with 'and').

questions.q: As much as 3,000 Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel have been deployed in (A)/various parts of Hisar district in Haryana and roads have been barricaded on Monday to stop (B)/ protestors from reaching the residence of IG Hisar Range. (C)/ The protest is against the criminal cases that have been filed against more than 350 farmers last week. (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'as much as'→'as many as' (used for countable nouns like personnel).

questions.q: Neither my sister (A)/ nor my mother (B)/ were able to help me (C)/ in doing household chores (D)/ No error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C
With neither...nor, verb agrees with the nearest subject ('mother' = singular). 'were'→'was'.

questions.q: Radha always wanted to be a soldier, (A)/ but her grades at school weren't (B)/quite enough good to get her (C)/ into army school (D)/ No Error (E)
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'quite enough good'→'quite good enough' (enough follows the adjective it modifies).

questions.q: In the following questions, a sentence is provided which is divided in different parts. One of these parts may or may not be grammatically or contextually correct. Choose the option corresponding to the error as part of your answer.
opts: A petition was filed in the Supreme Court on Monday seeking to quash/
an FIRs filed by Delhi Police against 24 persons arrested in the/
capital for putting over posters critical of Prime Minister Modi/
and his government over them is handling of the/
Covid pandemic and the national vaccination policy.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'putting over'→'putting up' (put up = display a notice/poster).

questions.q: In the following questions, a sentence is provided which is divided in different parts. One of these parts may or may not be grammatically or contextually correct. Choose the option corresponding to the error as part of your answer.
opts: The Bombay High Court bench of Justices Shahrukh Kathawalla and Surendra Tawade/
was scathing against Navi Mumbai police when it was/
brought to their notice that the advocate arrested/
in an allegedly extortion and kid-napping case was/
hand cuffed and his arrest was close to illegal detention
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'allegedly' (adverb)→'alleged' (adjective), needed to modify the noun phrase.

questions.q: In the following questions, a sentence is provided which is divided in different parts. One of these parts may or may not be grammatically or contextually correct. Choose the option corresponding to the error as part of your answer.
opts: Maharastra Health Minister Rajesh Tope on Thursday told/
Prime Minister Narendra Modi that mucormycosis or blackfungus/
which has claimed 90 lives in the State so far, is a matter of prime concern/
for it at present, and the State Government need/
more supply of the medicine used in its treatment.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
Singular subject 'the State Government' needs singular verb 'needs', not 'need'.

questions.q: In the following questions, a sentence is provided which is divided in different parts. One of these parts may or may not be grammatically or contextually correct. Choose the option corresponding to the error as part of your answer.
opts: Prime Minister Narendra Modi at Thursday asked officials/
to record the spread of corona virus infection and its/
seriousness between the youth and children, and cautioned that the/
challenge posed by COVID-19 will remain as long it is/
present even at a "minor scale" in the country.
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'at Thursday'→'on Thursday' (days of the week take preposition 'on').

questions.q: In the following questions, a sentence is provided which is divided in different parts. One of these parts may or may not be grammatically or contextually correct. Choose the option corresponding to the error as part of your answer.
opts: has gained significance in the COVID/
times with players spending lot of time in bio-bubbles,/
Tendulkar said acceptability is the key/
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts. From the given six parts, two parts are error free and the remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which does not contain any error.

Just when the spread of COVID19 had reached (A)/ catastrophic proportions, with the daily (B)/ caseload rising faster than that seen anywhere near (C)/ in the world for the beginning of the (D)/ novel corona virus pandemic, the Government of India has (E)/ acted by unveiling a completely revamp vaccine strategy. (F)/
questions.sol:
Answer: D — BE
A: 'had'→'has' | C: 'near' is redundant | D: 'for'→'since' | F: 'revamp'→'revamped'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts. From the given six parts, two parts are error free and the remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which does not contain any error.

Since last year's (A)/ Oscar sweep by (B)/ Parasite, there are those who (C)/ peddled the myth which (D)/ lightning would not strike (E)/ the same place twice.(F)/
questions.sol:
Answer: E — BF
A: 'Since'→'After' | C: 'are'→'were' | D: 'which'→'that' | E: 'would'→'will'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts. From the given six parts, two parts are error free and the remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which does not contain any error.

Though some form or other of the lockdown (A)/ has been imposed in the worst interests (B)/ of the health and safety of the common men, (C)/ there is otherwise a direly need to (D)/ ramp up medical facilities to meet the (E)/ needs of both symptomatic and a symptomatic patients. (F)/
questions.sol:
Answer: A — EF
A: 'the'→'a' | B: 'worst'→'best' | C: 'men'→'man' | D: 'direly'→'dire'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts. From the given six parts, two parts are error free and the remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which does not contain any error.

The decisions by the Tamil Nadu government (A)/ to augment oxygen produce utilising the (B)/ facilities at Vedanta's Sterlite copper plant (C)/at Tuticorin should only be (D)/ seen as a pragmatic wager, when (E)/ the nation is gasping on oxygen.(F)/
questions.sol:
Answer: B — CE
A: 'decisions'→'decision' | B: 'produce'→'production' | D: 'at'→'in' | F: 'on'→'for'

questions.q:
A sentence is divided into six parts. From the given six parts, two parts are error free and the remaining four parts are erroneous. You have to find that combination of two parts which does not contain any error.

The oxygen shortages at hospitals treating (A)/ COVID-19 patients led to multiple deaths, (B)/ which has turned the spotlight above the production (C)/ and supply of the life sustained element, (D)/ may start easing in the coming weeks, (E)/ challenges notwithstanding, officials say. (F)/
questions.sol:
Answer: D — AE
B: 'led'→'leading' | C: 'above'→'on' | D: 'sustained'→'sustaining'

questions.q:
The following questions consist of a sentence which is divided into five parts. The portion specified in bold suggests that it is grammatically correct and does not require any correction or replacement. One of the other parts may or may not contain an error. The options given show the part containing an error. If the given sentence is grammatically correct or does not require any correction, choose "No error" as your answer.

Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman assured the States(a)/ of a seamless supply of medical oxygen, even as (b)/pharmaceutical manufacturers told the government that (c) /they will ramp over production of Remdesivir (d) /injections from 36 lakh vials a month to 1 crore(e).
questions.sol:
Answer: C — d
'ramp over'→'ramp up' (ramp up = increase production).

questions.q: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will join other global leaders (a)/ to participate in the Leaders' Summit on Climate (b)/ a virtual event that will reflect (c)/the concerns of U.S. President (d)/Joe Biden regarding climate change(e).
questions.sol:
Answer: B — c
'that'→'which' (non-defining relative clause describing the event).

questions.q: The Congress criticised the Centre over differential (a)/pricing of the Covishield vaccine, with former (b)/ Union minister Jairam Ramesh calling it "atrocious" that the (c)/ Centre could procure them for 150 per dose (d)/but States would have to pay 400 per dose(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — d
'them'→'it' (referring to the singular vaccine).

questions.q: The Central government told the Bombay High Court(a)/ on Wednesday that door to door vaccination(b)/ against the COVID19 virus is not(c)/ possible as it was expected to(d)/leading to contamination and wastage (e).
questions.sol:
Answer: D — e
'leading'→'lead' (after 'expected to', base form is needed).

questions.q: Officials said a local shipping company, handling (a) /the vessel sailing under the flag of Antigua and Barbuda, from (b) / Rotterdam in the Netherlands to China cited a "mechanical emergency" (c)/ and sought permission from the harbour (d) /manager at the China run Hambantota Port (e).
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No error
The sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q: Sri Lanka on Wednesday marked the second anniversary (a) / of the Easter terror bombings that killed more than(b)/ 250 people in April 2019, amid mounting criticism by the Catholic Church(c)/ and political opposition over the delay in (d)/completion of the probe into the incident (e).
questions.sol:
Answer: B — c
'by'→'from' (criticism from a body/group, not by).

questions.q:
Read the following sentence to find out whether there is any error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. The option corresponding to that part will be your answer. Ignore the errors of punctuation if any.

An expert panel of the country's (A)/ top drug regulator has allowed Bharat Biotech (B)/ to administer a third dose of Covaxin (C)/ to some of the volunteers in its outgoing (D)/ clinical trials of the Covid vaccine(E).
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'outgoing'→'ongoing' (ongoing = in progress; outgoing = extrovert or departing).

questions.q: The brooding tension between Punjab University and its students(A)/ over the closing of the institution's (B)/ A C Joshi Library came to a head as(C)/ almost a hundred students broke a lock(D)/ to enter the outer section of the reading room(E).
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'brooding'→'brewing' (brewing tension = tension building up).

questions.q: As tens of thousands of migrants returned to there villages (A)/ following the outbreak of the Covid-19 pandemic and (B)/ the national lockdown that followed last year, (C)/ the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS) (D)/ emerged as a safety net for labourers in rural areas (E).
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'there'→'their' (possessive pronoun).

questions.q: Prime Minister Narendra Modi called (A)/ off Chief Minister Uddhav Thackeray (B)/ and enquired about the health of (C)/ his wife Rashmi, the editor of (D)/Shiv Sena mouthpiece Saamana(E).
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'called off'→'called up' (call up = phone someone; call off = cancel).

questions.q: Indian Army chief General MM Naravane asserted India(A)/ has "not lost out on any territory" in the months-long military(B)/ standby with China in eastern Ladakh, adding that "we are where we were" (C)/ after the worst border conflict between the two nuclear-armed(D)/neighbours in decades, news agency ANI reported (E).
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'standby'→'standoff' (standoff = deadlock; standby = backup/reserve).

questions.q:
Following are the sentences in which a passage is divided into four/five statements or it could be independent statements; find the sentences with an error in the following questions

a) Petrol price on Tuesday crossed Rs 99 a litre mark in Mumbai after oil companies raised petrol and diesel rates on surge in international oil prices.
b) Petrol price was increased by 27 paise per litre and diesel by 29 paise, according to a price notification of state-owned fuel retailers.
c) The tenth hike this month, pushed petrol and diesel prices to an all-time high across the country.
d) In Delhi, petrol rates climbed from Rs 92.85 a litre and diesel rose to Rs 83.51.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — only d
'climbed from'→'climbed to' (a limit is specified, not a range).

questions.q:
a) The year 2021 has been a blockbuster for private companies.
b) E-commerce stole much of the thunder as their valuations shot up during the pandemic.
c) This is due to the massive demand for food delivery, video conferencing, digital tools, and online shopping.
d) In the US and Chinese stock markets, technology companies are producing massive profits for their investors.
e) Now it looks like India is going to join the race too.
questions.sol: Answer: E — All are correct

questions.q:
a) Honda motorcycle and Scooter India announced that its 125 cc motorcycle, the Honda Shine, crossed the sales milestone of 90 lakh units since its launch in 2006.
b) Piaggio India launched the Aprilia SXR 160 in the country, priced at Rs 1.26 lakh (ex-showroom, Pune), making it the most expensive offering from the brand in the country.
c) Suzuki Motorcycles has also announced to hike prices across its product range but is yet to disclose the quantum or percentage of the price hike.
d) Former minister and Congress leader HC Mahadevappa stated that the Central government's failure to contain Delhi riots will affect the country image globally.
questions.sol:
Answer: A — only d
'country image'→'country\'s image' (possessive apostrophe missing).

questions.q:
a) A number of overseas stars is expected to pull out of the inaugural edition of the IPL due to a packed schedule and international commitments.
b) West Indies, Pakistan, Australia will all be in action in July this year and as a result, their availability in the competition would be limited.
c) Two Australian women players -- Rachael Haynes and Jess Jonassen- have already withdrawn from the women's tournament due to quarantine requirements, reported ESPN cricinfo.
d) The future life span of Earth's oxygen-rich atmosphere is approximately one billion years, a new study reveals.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — only a
'A number of' takes plural verb. 'is expected'→'are expected'.

questions.q:
a) Rates had already crossed the Rs 100-mark in several cities in Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra and with the latest increase, the price in Mumbai too was inching towards that level.
b) A litre of petrol in Mumbai now comes for Rs 99.14 and diesel is priced at Rs 90.71 per litre.
c) Fuel prices differ from state to state depending on the incidence of local taxes such as VAT and freight charges.
d) Rajasthan levies highest value-added tax (VAT) on petrol in the country, followed by Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra.
e) The hike comes as Brent crude oil price rose above USD 70 per barrel for the first time since March
questions.sol:
Answer: D — only d
Superlative 'highest' must be preceded by article 'the': 'the highest'.

questions.q:
In each of the questions given below, a sentence is divided into several parts. Each part of the sentence is marked and may contain a grammatical error. Identify the parts of the sentence which are grammatically correct.

After a SARS-CoV-2 infection, a (A)/ person's antibody and T cell (B)/ responses may be stronger enough to (C)/ provide protection to reinfection. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — A-B
C: 'stronger'→'strong' (enough follows positive degree) | D: 'to'→'against' ('protection against').

questions.q: The theory behind wearing two pairs (A)/ of socks is that it creates a situation where (B)/ your foot is rubbing against another sock, instead of rubbing (C)/ direct against the inside material of the shoe. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — B-C
A: 'two pairs'→'two pair' (definite numeral + pair = singular) | D: 'direct'→'directly'.

questions.q: George Russell was exceptional (A)/ good in qualifying in 2020, often putting (B)/ in one-lap performances those (C)/ put the car into Q2 ahead of their quicker rivals. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — B-D
A: 'exceptional'→'exceptionally' (adverb modifying adjective) | C: 'those'→'that'.

questions.q: A person can develop immunity (A)/ - the ability to resist infection - from (B)/ being infected with a virus (C)/ or from getting a vaccine. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No Error
The sentence is entirely grammatically correct.

questions.q: Taj Rishikesh Resort and Spa in (A)/ Uttarakhand's Rishikesh has been (B)/ closed at least three days after (C)/ 76 peoples tested positive for coronavirus. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — A-B
C: missing 'for' before 'three days' | D: 'peoples'→'people'.

questions.q:
In the following question, there are two sentences in each question and each sentence has been divided into four parts that may have an error. From both the sentences find out which part has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'E'.

(I) An oxygen tanker had reached to the hospital (A)/ after the staff spent hours running around (B)/ in search of supplies and making (C)/ frantic calls to the government and police.(D)

(II) The hospital ran out (A)/ of oxygen in the afternoon (B)/ and the supply from (C)/ the vendor never come. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — (i)-A, (ii)-D
(i): 'reached to'→'reached' (omit 'to') | (ii): 'come'→'came'.

questions.q:
(I) Chandigarh's women entrepreneurs (A)/ are reshaping the (B)/ tricity food business in Covid times (C)/ with optimism and grit. (D)

(II) Italy has joined other countries by imposing (A)/ restrictions on travel from India (B)/ to avert the spreading of a COVID-19 variant (C)/ as the Asian nation struggles with a surge in infections.(D)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — (i)-C, (ii)-C
(i): 'tricity'→'tricity\'s' | (ii): 'spreading'→'spread'.

questions.q:
(I) From sharing stories of funny excuses given by people (A)/ to bypass lockdown norms to spreading awareness about (B)/ Covid-appropriate behaviour, Pune Police have been used the micro-blogging site (C)/ to engage with the people of the city.(D)

(II) Home chefs who have volunteered (A)/ to cook and severe Covid patients have been flooded (B)/ with distress calls from entire families who have (C)/ tested positive or from those living alone. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — (i)-C, (ii)-B
(i): 'have been used'→'have been using' | (ii): 'severe'→'serve'.

questions.q:
(I) The district health authorities, widely appreciated (A)/ for running almost 30 Covid-19 sampling centres each day (B)/ for testing people from across and outside (C)/ the district, has reduced the number of sampling centres. (D)

(II) The administration also announced (A)/ free Covid-19 vaccination (B)/ in the Union Territory of people (C)/ between 18 and 45 years. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — (i)-D, (ii)-C
(i): 'has'→'have' (plural subject 'authorities') | (ii): 'of'→'for'.

questions.q:
(I) Mathura district administration issued notices to the ITIs (A)/ on the basis of an enquiry conducted by (B)/ the UP Social Welfare Department which found involvement (C)/ of these ITIs in fraudulent disbursement of crores of rupees.(D)

(II) According to a recent World Bank report, (A)/ 10 billion doses should be sufficient (B)/ to achieve globally equity in the distribution of vaccines (C)/ and attain worldwide herd immunity by March 2022. (D)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — (i)-B, (ii)-C
(i): 'enquiry'→'inquiry' (formal investigation) | (ii): 'globally'→'global' (adjective needed).

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'rather'→'yet' (with although/though, the correlative is 'yet', not 'rather').

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'expert'→'expertise' (noun needed: 'Indian health expertise').

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'at all the verticals'→'across all the verticals' (wrong preposition).

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
Singular subject 'threat' needs 'was', not 'were'.

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No Error
The sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'all season'→'every season' (correct quantifier usage).

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
opts: The UP duo lost the first
game but did well to putting
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'to putting'→'to put' (parallel with 'to look' later in the sentence).

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'collapse'→'collapsed' (must match past tense 'became').

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
opts: as a priority in the NEP 2020
questions.sol:
Answer: D
Plural subject 'teachers' needs 'have', not 'has'.

questions.q: The sentences given below have been broken into five parts. One of these might be erroneous and need to be corrected. You must read the same and choose the respective part as your answer.
opts: occupy the hostel rely on a water
tanker that comes once every two
days and costs Rs 5,000 per tanker.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No Error
The sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) In an extraordinary move
(B) the student council find
(C) two of their own members
(D) for being absent from meetings.
questions.sol:
Answer: A — (B) and (C)
B: 'find'→'fined' | C: 'their'→'its' (student council is singular).

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) Before disposing of
(B) his property,
(C) he met his counsel
(D) and took his advise.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — (A) and (D)
A: 'disposing of'→'disposing off' | D: 'advise'→'advice'.

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) He was
(B) puzzled about
(C) and concerned
(D) about her behaviour.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — (B) only
'puzzled about'→'puzzled by' (puzzled always collocates with 'by').

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) The report is not only designed to present
(B) the problems faced by us,
(C) but also recommend possible
(D) solutions to these problems.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — (A) and (C)
A: 'not only designed to present'→'designed not only to present' | C: 'also recommend'→'also to recommend'.

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) My friend has
(B) beauty
(C) intelligence and
(D) enough of money
questions.sol:
Answer: D — (D) only
'enough of money'→'enough money' (superfluous 'of').

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) The lifeguard felt that the boys
(B) were in danger because
(C) they swum out
(D) too far.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — (C) only
'they swum out'→'they had swum out' (past perfect needed).

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) As it turned out,
(B) Yusuf and myself
(C) were recommended
(D) for promotion.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — (B) only
'Yusuf and myself'→'Yusuf and I' (reflexive pronoun incorrect as subject).

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) I greatly admired Sameer
(B) giving in to
(C) his brother
(D) in order to prevent a fight.
questions.sol:
Answer: A — (A) only
'I greatly admired Sameer'→'I greatly admired Sameer\'s' (possessive needed before gerund phrase).

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) She is my class teacher
(B) and she didn't came
(C) to the program
(D) day before yesterday.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — (B) only
'didn't came'→'didn't come' (after auxiliary 'did', base form is needed).

questions.q:
Give below are sentences each of which have been broken into four parts. One or two or more parts have an error. Each sentence is then followed by 5 options that show the parts/parts that are grammatically INCORRECT. Mark the right option as your answer.

(A) My grand-father was
(B) reading TΟΙ
(C) newspaper and
(D) he found scared news.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — (B) and (D)
B: needs article 'the' before TOI | D: 'scared news'→'scary news'.

questions.q:
In each of the following questions, a sentence has been divided into five parts out of which one part is erroneous. You are required to choose the option which is grammatically or contextually incorrect.

After the BJP slammed West Bengal (a)/ CM over her silence on the recently attacks against (b)/ the Hindu community in Bangladesh, (c)/All India Trinamool Congress leader asked why (d)/ Narendra Modi had not made a public statement on the same.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: B — b
'recently' (adverb) → 'recent' (adjective needed before noun 'attacks').

questions.q: As the cumulative vaccine doses administered (a)/ in India surpassed the 100-crore mark, (b)/ Union Health Minister Mansukh Mandaviya launched a song and (c)/ a film describe the efforts that went behind (d)/ the launch of the world's largest Covid inoculation programme.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — d
'describe' → 'describing' (present participle needed).

questions.q: Union Home Minister Amit Shah conducted an (a)/ aerial survey of rain-ravaged areas (b)/ of Uttarakhand to assessing the (c)/ damage which the state government (d)/ has pegged at Rs 7,000 crore.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — c
'to assessing' → 'to assess' (to + V1 structure).

questions.q: After the Durga Puja (a)/ celebrations ended (b)/ in West Bengal, the number (c)/ of Covid-19 infections (d)/ are on the rise in the state.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — e
'are' → 'is' (the number of + singular verb).

questions.q: Several foreign dignitary, (a)/ including Sri Lankan minister Namal Rajapaksa, (b)/ hailed the inauguration of the (c)/ Kushinagar International Airport, and said it will (d)/ boost the number of Buddhist tourists visiting India.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — a
'dignitary' → 'dignitaries' (several + plural noun).

questions.q: As much as 150 Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) (a)/ which were upgraded by (b)/ Tata Technologies Limited under (c)/ Karnataka's UDYOGA program are set (d)/ to be inaugurated in two weeks.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: A — a
'As much as' → 'As many as' (used with countable nouns).

questions.q: As India achieved a major milestone in its (a)/ vaccination programme by administering over (b)/100-crore Covid-19 vaccines in the country on Thursday, (c)/ Uttar Pradesh has started preparing to vaccinate children (d)/ from the ages of two to 18 years.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: D — d
'started preparing' → 'started preparations' (noun form needed).

questions.q: The Assam Congress staged (a)/ a state-wide protest over the alleged failure (b)/ of the BJP-lead government to check (c)/ the rising prices of fuels (d)/ and other essential commodities.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — c
'BJP-lead' → 'BJP-led' (past participle used as adjective).

questions.q: Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra (a)/ announced on Thursday that all class 12th (b)/ passed girls will be given a smartphone (c)/ while all graduate girls will get an electric scooty (d)/ when her party forms a government in Uttar Pradesh.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: E — e
'when' → 'if' (conditional clause, not a certainty).

questions.q: From the Qutub Minar in Delhi to (a)/ Golconda Fort in Hyderabad, (b)/ a total of 100 heritage monument were (c)/ illuminated in the colours of the national flag to mark (d)/ India's 100 crore Covid-19 vaccine milestone.(e)
questions.sol:
Answer: C — c
'monument' → 'monuments' (plural needed after 100).

questions.q:
In the following questions, five sentences are given among which one or more than one sentence contains error. Choose the sentence/s that contain/s error using the given alternatives.

A. Scientists believe that the cow is the only animal which both inhales and exhales oxygen.
B. There were food shortages and other deprivations during the Civil War.
C. The article provides a useful summary of the current state of play in the negotiations.
D. The old railway station was in a state of dereliction.
E. Unless 1951, China had no ongoing suzerainty over Tibet.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — Both A and E
A: 'only animal which' → 'only animal that' | E: 'Unless' → 'Until'.

questions.q:
A. They invited her to speak, especially because of her experience in inner cities.
B. Taxes can be designed either as revenue neutral nor revenue augmenting.
C. Alcohol should be consumed in moderation.
D. I don't believe these exam results are a true reflection of your ability.
E. The allegations, if true, could lead to her resignation.
questions.sol:
Answer: A — Only B
'either...nor' → 'either...or' (correct correlative conjunction pair).

questions.q:
A. The police are trying to determine the series of events that led up to the murder.
B. This year's Olympic Games will be a biggest ever sporting event.
C. This is one of the worst natural disaster ever to befall the area.
D. She declared that it was the best chocolate cake she had ever tasted.
E. The increasing demand for private cars could be halted by more investment in public transport.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — Both B and C
B: 'a biggest' → 'the biggest' (superlative needs 'the') | C: 'disaster' → 'disasters' (one of the + plural).

questions.q:
A. She glanced for the room to see who was there.
B. This type of nursery care may well be the best choice for your child.
C. This booklet provides useful information about local services.
D. Everyone has there own ideas about the best way to bring up children.
E. She was awarded a medal for showing supreme courage.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — Both A and D
A: 'glanced for' → 'glanced around' | D: 'there' → 'their'.

questions.q:
A. Unable to swim, he watched helplessly as the child struggled desperately in the water.
B. The company has threatened to cut my hours at work.
C. Residents watched helpless as water rushed into their homes.
D. People in executive roles often work long hours.
E. Police are investigating allegations of corruption involving senior executives.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Only C
'watched helpless' → 'watched helplessly' (adverb needed after verb 'watched').

questions.q:
A. The two groups of scientists are vying to get funding for their research projects.
B. This group of chemicals are known to be harmful to people with asthma.
C. Tickets are sold well for upcoming concert tour.
D. Employment levels are unlikely to improve before the end of the year.
E. The number of business failures have risen steeply last year.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — Both B and E
B: 'group...are' → 'group...is' (singular subject) | E: 'have risen' → 'rose' (past tense needed).

questions.q:
A. The government is expected to call an election very soon.
B. They have been engaged in a legal battle with the council since several months.
C. He has established him as the leading candidate in the election.
D. The work has to be finished by next Tuesday.
E. It's important to create a good impression when you meet a new client.
questions.sol:
Answer: D — Both B and C
B: 'since' → 'for' (periods of time use 'for') | C: 'him' → 'himself' (reflexive pronoun needed).

questions.q:
A. He was the first member of his regiment to die while on active service.
B. The coach insists that all team members must attend early-morning practice.
C. She has been studying for her doctorate for three years already.
D. I want time to study this contract through before signing it.
E. Scientists have established the relationship between lung cancer or smoking.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — Both D and E
D: 'through' → 'thoroughly' | E: 'between...or' → 'between...and'.

questions.q:
A. Much people are concerned about the destruction of the rainforests.
B. Opposition groups plan to stage mass demonstrations all over the country.
C. The industry workers have been subjected to reduced hours and the potential for layoffs.
D. The impact of the new legislation have been greatly overstated.
E. If you can help us in a small way, it would be greatly appreciated.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — Both A and D
A: 'Much' → 'Many' (countable noun) | D: 'have been' → 'has been' (singular subject 'impact').

questions.q:
A. The tests show that the tumour has decreased in size since we started treatment.
B. The king went on exile because of the political situation in his country.
C. Britain and France declared war on Germany in 1939 as a result of the invasion of Poland.
D. We have just appointed a coordinator whom will oversee the whole project.
E. Her latest project is a film based on the life of a 19th-century music hall star.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — Both B and D
B: 'on exile' → 'into exile' | D: 'whom' → 'who' (subject of relative clause).

questions.q:
Each of the following sentences is divided into parts, which are marked a, b, c and d. One of these parts may contain an error. Mark that part as your answer from the options given below and choose 'e' if you find out there is no error.

A spokesman for World Bank in India said the a)/ discussions with the government were in the early b)/ stages, adding that farther rounds of consultations c)/ will help us define the contours of this collaboration d)/ no error e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: C — c
'farther' (physical distance) → 'further' (additional/more).

questions.q: To mitigate such risks for foreign banks, the a)/ loans would be given sovereign guarantees and being routed b)/ through Indian government agencies such as the c)/ Small Industries Development Bank of India, the first official said d)/ no error e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: B — b
'being routed' → 'be routed' (would + be + V3 passive structure).

questions.q: In an interview, Rajan confirmed he hadn't a)/ sought the position and cite the fact that b)/ central banking has become much more political c)/ in recent times as an explanation for why not d)/ no error e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: B — b
'cite' → 'cited' (had + V3 structure needed).

questions.q: He left India Central Bank after just a)/ one term amid heavy criticism from segments b)/ of the government for offering opinions c)/ on matters unrelated to monetary policy d)/ no error e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: A — a
'India Central Bank' → 'India\'s Central Bank' (possessive needed).

questions.q: ISRO has not yet officially disclosed the a)/ nature of the glitch, though some news reports b)/ have indicated that the main cryogenic engine c)/ failed to built pressure due to helium gas leakage d)/ no error e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: D — d
'to built' → 'to build' (to + V1 structure).

questions.q: The two sides confirmed they had a)/ discussed increased Chinese imports of the U.S. b)/ agricultural products, which have fallen c)/ dramatically this year, and that d)/ they would meet again in Washington e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: A — b
'increased' → 'increasing' (present participle as adjective needed).
