questions.q: Rising economic uncertainties, a)/ slowdown in fresh investments and b)/ stress in the non-bank lending c)/ space damp demand from d)/ both urban and rural consumers e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: C — d
'damp' → 'damped' (past participle acting as adjective).

questions.q: The high insurance costs, rise in taxes and a)/ liquidity crunch in the non-banking finance b)/ segment, tightening of lending c)/ norms have significantly affected d)/ the domestic sales in the last few months e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: A — b
'in the non-banking finance' → 'across the non-banking finance' (correct preposition).

questions.q: The lenders have reservations a)/ about releasing a month's salary b)/ for Jet Airways' staffs as sought c)/ by several worker unions, said another d)/ person aware of the matter e)/
questions.sol:
Answer: D — c
'staffs' → 'staff' (staff is uncountable, no plural form).

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: We keep going to the food hall at the boys' hostel which is nearly 2 km away.
This will galvanize Al ecosystem and nurture quality human resources in the field.
The industry will also be encouraged to invest in research and development.
The team will visit 5 districts and review there functioning in 33 parameters.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'there'→'their' (possessive pronoun needed).

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: They have neither confirmed nor denied their relationship.
The boulders were transported from Janakpur, said to be the birthplace of Sita.
This is causing Indian users to shift investments overseas.
Musk had "confidence in the available of funding".
The ASI was taken into custody over the matter earlier.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'available' (adjective)→'availability' (noun needed after 'in the').

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: Police will exhume the body of a 30-year-old migrant worker who was buried discreetly by his employer
Adani Group's cumulative market capitalisation losses have hit $100 billion.
You posting your Call letter is kind of motivation we all aspirants need.
India stand-in captain Hardik Pandya said he has always enjoyed hitting sixes.
questions.sol:
Answer: E — All are correct
All sentences are grammatically correct.

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: This comes following multiple incidents of people using 'Koyta' to commit crimes.
The driver ran away from the spot following the accident.
Five officers were charged last week over his death.
Prithvi had not feature in a single match in the series.
The board decided it wouldn't be "morally correct" to proceed with the FPO.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'had not feature'→'had not featured' (past perfect needs V3).

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: The donation is happening over a year after Buck's death.
Scaringe reported apologised to employees for the necessity of the cuts in the email.
The road will be widened up to 18.30 m to ease traffic movement.
The Fordow plant enriches uranium to near weapons-grade levels.
questions.sol:
Answer: B
'reported'→'reportedly' (adverb needed to qualify the verb 'apologised').

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: The revision in tax rates under the new regime will augur well on the consumption
It's customary for the finance minister to address the RBI board.
Correct cricket never goes out of fashion.
The driver of the truck carrying sugarcane fledded the the spot.
questions.sol:
Answer: D
'fledded'→'fled' (past tense of 'flee' is 'fled', not 'fledded').

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: The move comes amid rising tensions in wake of the recent protests.
Shaakuntalam', which is based on a play by Kalidasa, is expected to release this year.
Trade unions demanded the government give increased wages.
India has a very broad spectrum of infrastructure companies
questions.sol:
Answer: E — No Error
All sentences are grammatically correct.

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: The couple could not get out as their seat belt got entangled.
The foreign delegates were welcomed into the UNESCO World Heritage Site by the district administration.
No injury were reported in the incident.
The accused who was under the influence of alcohol has been suspended.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'No injury were'→'No injuries were' OR 'No injury was' (subject-verb mismatch).

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: Musk added the issues should get addressed by next week.
Three persons have been arrested in connection with the incident.
Byju's has laid over another 1,000-1,200 employees in the latest cost-cutting initiative
The auto driver took a U-turn from a median due to a traffic jam.
questions.sol:
Answer: C
'laid over'→'laid off' ('lay off' = dismiss employees; 'lay over' = stopover during travel).

questions.q: The questions given below carry sentences, one of which might be erroneous. You must read them carefully and choose the sentence with an error as your answer.
opts: An injury on his left hand kept him away for the game.
They had no choice other than violating the restrictions imposed.
He revealed he has not seen more than two MCU films.
Twitter has begun the appeals process for suspended accounts.
questions.sol:
Answer: A
'kept him away for'→'kept him away from' (away from something, not away for).

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. After being told of the many errors, he said several times that he strived to recollect events six years afterward.
B. However, recessionary pressures, the global meltdown, tumbling stock markets and job cuts have worsened the economic climate.
C. In spite of all the positive developments, the region is politically unstable and riven.
D. Only if a similar dose of positive news keeps flowing in from overseas shores in the forthcoming week, we could see the market keep up the good work.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — A only
A: 'strived'→'strove' (irregular past tense of 'strive').

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. Even as the profile of the posh-nosh buyer of art gets a makeover, India's wealthy do not want to be seen as buying art for investment.
B. They want to reinvent their identity all over again.
C. "Art suddenly is in search for a fresh, new purpose," says Harsh Goenka.
D. "Most of the buyers in the older time bought it for the passion for art in them."
questions.sol:
Answer: B — C only
C: 'in search for'→'in search of'.

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. Art is trying to get its soul back.
B. There's a renewed passion, even as the art market swings between high and low.
C. The new breed of collector is creating arty spaces for an arty dialogue.
D. It's dare game in the art world.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — C only
C: 'The new breed of collector'→'collectors' (breed as a group needs plural).

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. Anjum Singh, all of 41, is experimenting with a new informality in her art.
B. The strokes of contradictions are visible of her paintings.
C. Unlike her parents, she's moving beyond the world of landscapes and paints.
D. "I'm more experimenting, that's why I'm also doing sculptures and installations."
questions.sol:
Answer: D — B and D
B: 'visible of'→'visible in' | D: 'more experimenting'→'experimentative'.

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. His best role was as 'Frodo' in 'The Lord of the Rings'.
B. Elijah then carefully chose varied roles in critically-acclaimed films like 'Bobby', 'Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind' and 'Sin City'.
C. He provided the voice of the main character, 'Mumble', in the award-winning animated film 'Happy Feet'.
D. "Smashing Pumpkins' is Elijah's favourite band."
questions.sol:
Answer: B — B only
B: 'films like'→'films such as' (formal listing of examples uses 'such as').

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. This and the willful withdrawal from public scrutiny have been some time coming, Karan admits.
B. "If you see the graph, there is a shift from idealism to cynicism," he says.
C. "I reached a zone after 'Kabhi Alvida Na Kehna' where I just need to be."
D. "I want to make life more about me than it's been before."
questions.sol:
Answer: D — C and D
C: 'need to be'→'needed to be' (past context).

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. He has wronged you by not making good his promise.
B. You should have at least for an explanation.
C. If it were up to me, he would not have got away Scot-free.
D. How does his conscious allow him such things?
questions.sol:
Answer: B — B and C
B: 'at least for'→'at least sought' | C: missing the phrase 'asked for' or similar verb.

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. Winds blew up to 45 mph, with gusts reaching 70 mph in midday.
B. They were forecasted to diminish in the evening before roaring over 60 mph after 11 pm.
C. "This fire has the real potential of moving from where it is now... as far as Pacific Coast," said LA county fire chief.
D. Residents downwind were warned to remain alert into the night.
questions.sol:
Answer: B — A and B
A: 'in midday'→'at midday' | B: 'forecasted'→'forecast' (irregular verb).

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. Boring, drab, dull — you can pick any of these adjectives to describe Sunday's Nanoli Stud Pune Derby.
B. Not even in the blue riband event people were jumping out of their seats to cheer on the Pesi Shroff trained winner.
C. The lack of interest for such a huge event was baffling.
D. The turnout was far less than the previous years.
questions.sol:
Answer: C — B only
B: 'people were'→'were people' (inverted structure needed after negative adverbial 'Not even...').

questions.q:
Each question consists of four sentences. Some sentences are grammatically incorrect or inappropriate. Select the option that indicates the grammatically incorrect and inappropriate sentence(s).

A. The dress should be as good or better than the one you picked last time.
B. The event is a very important one.
C. Your relatives will be there in full attendance.
D. You can also expect some ex-classmates.
questions.sol:
Answer: A — A only
A: 'as good or better than'→'as good as or better than' (incomplete comparison).
